# Test error handling and edge cases

# Test timeout handling with very aggressive timeouts
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=1s
env MCP_BASH_TIMEOUT=2s

# Test command that should fail
! exec nonexistent_command_that_fails 2>/dev/null

# Test invalid tool execution
! exec non_existent_command --help 2>/dev/null

# Test invalid JSON handling
exec bash -c 'echo "{\"invalid\": json, \"missing\": quotes}" > malformed.json'

# mcpspy should handle malformed JSON gracefully (may output warnings)
exec bash -c 'cat malformed.json | mcpspy --pretty || true'
# Should not crash, may produce warnings

# Test empty input
exec bash -c 'true' | mcpspy --pretty
# Should handle empty input gracefully

# Test server connection failures
! exec go run non_existent_server.go 2>/dev/null

# Test rapid command succession under aggressive timeouts
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=200ms
exec echo "test1"
stdout 'test1'
exec echo "test2"  
stdout 'test2'
exec echo "test3"
stdout 'test3'

# Cleanup
exec rm -f malformed.json
